title: DPI argument type incorrectly recognized
body:
Hi,

Verilator fails with following code:

```
module t_dpi_args;

    parameter P_INPUT_DATA_LENGTH_MAX = 1000;

    byte unsigned input_data[];
    byte unsigned input_data_max[];

    import "DPI-C" function int my_func(input byte unsigned dataIn[P_INPUT_DATA_LENGTH_MAX]);

    initial
    begin
        int result;
        input_data_max = new[P_INPUT_DATA_LENGTH_MAX](input_data);
        result = my_func(input_data_max);
    end

endmodule;
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  
(Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t_dpi_args.sv:15:26: Argument is not an unpacked array while parameter 'dataIn' is
                                       : ... note: In instance 't_dpi_args'
   15 |         result = my_func(input_data_max);
      |                          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.042
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
verilator --cc t_dpi_args.sv
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

5.042, no I have not tried latest master yet. Again, this works in VCS.

What OS and distribution are you using?

```
Linux runner4.tropic.dev.hq.sldev.cz 4.18.0-553.58.1.el8_10.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Jun 12 14:27:07 EDT 2025 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

```
Yes, but again no guarantee I can fix this.
```
